name: Manoa Honey & Mead
tagline: Hawaii's only meadery — taste local honey wines, tour the apiary, and learn about beekeeping on a working honey farm.
bestTime: Saturday for Bees to Mead tours at 10am or 2pm (reservations required). Weekday afternoons for unhurried tastings
talkStory: Founded by beekeeper-turned-meadmaker Steve Myhre, this tiny Wahiawa meadery is Hawaii's only producer of mead (honey wine). They maintain their own hives and produce six varieties from local wildflower honey. The Bees to Mead tour takes you through the working apiary before tasting. The industrial-park location surprises people, but the craft is serious -- their meads have won recognition at national mead competitions and the tasting room feels like discovering a well-kept secret.
